Title: Effects of altered α- and β-branch carotenoid biosynthesis on photoprotection and whole-plant acclimation of Arabidopsis to photo-oxidative stress

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enables qE component of NPQ and photoprotection under fluctuating light
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: photosystem II subunit S / light-harvesting complex superfamily-related photoprotective protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: protein level increased in mature leaves after 7 d sunfleck treatment in all genotypes except variable in lut2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sunfleck-induced photo-oxidative stress; fluctuating high-light pulses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PsbS protein content increased under sunflecks and was associated with enhanced NPQ in wild type and lut5, but increased PsbS alone was insufficient to enhance NPQ in npq1 lacking zeaxanthin formation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsbS levels increased in sunfleck-treated leaves of most genotypes. 2. Increased PsbS correlated with enhanced NPQ in wt and lut5. 3. In npq1, a ~40% increase in PsbS did not rescue NPQ up-regulation, showing that PsbS requires zeaxanthin-dependent machinery for full acclimatory function.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Western blot immunodetection; comparison across carotenoid mutants; chlorophyll fluorescence NPQ measurements
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NPQ1-dependent zeaxanthin pathway; PSII antenna complexes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: limited benefit if zeaxanthin synthesis is impaired
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the phenotype, PsbS is not the main manipulated gene in this paper; it is a measured downstream photoprotective component.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: leaf photoprotection; non-photochemical quenching; whole-plant acclimation to photo-oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes one end of lycopene to introduce an ε-ring, directing flux into the α-branch and enabling lutein biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sunfleck-induced photo-oxidative stress; fluctuating high-light pulses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of LUT2 causes lutein deficiency and accumulation of only β-branch carotenoids; under sunflecks carotenoid composition remains largely unchanged, NPQ is variable, and leaf mass per area increases.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lut2 mutants lack lutein and accumulate increased β-carotene and xanthophyll-cycle pigments. 2. Sunfleck treatment caused little change in carotenoid composition of lut2 compared with strong changes in other mutants. 3. lut2 showed variable PsbS and NPQ behavior and a significant increase in leaf mass per area under sunflecks.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; HPLC pigment profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence NPQ measurements; immunoblot of PsbS; growth phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: lutein deficiency; slower/erratic NPQ induction; altered leaf mass per area; possible sensitivity in acclimation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper studies the lut2 mutant phenotype rather than direct molecular characterization of the gene.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection; regulation of NPQ acclimation; photo-oxidative stress acclimation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: β-ring hydroxylation / balance between α- and β-branch carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates β-rings of carotenes to produce xanthophylls, contributing to lutein and other xanthophyll formation and controlling α/β branch balance
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; controls branch balance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sunfleck-induced photo-oxidative stress; fluctuating high-light pulses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Impairment of LUT5 causes high α/β carotenoid ratio with α-carotene accumulation and reduced xanthophylls; under sunflecks lutein and neoxanthin increase, chlorophyll declines strongly, and NPQ capacity is strongly up-regulated while lateral root formation is suppressed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lut5 mutants overaccumulate α-carotene at the expense of xanthophylls and β-carotene, producing a high α/β ratio. 2. Under sunflecks, lut5 showed the largest chlorophyll decline and increased lutein and neoxanthin, indicating strong acclimatory adjustment. 3. Despite reduced baseline NPQ, lut5 strongly up-regulated qE/NPQ during sunfleck stress and showed reduced lateral root formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for α/β branch balance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; HPLC pigment profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence NPQ measurements; PsbS immunoblot; leaf and root growth phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: high α/β ratio; stronger chlorophyll loss under stress; reduced lateral root formation; decreased leaf mass per area
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes HL up-regulation of a non-heme di-iron β-ring hydroxylase gene in Arabidopsis, supporting enzyme importance in stress-induced xanthophyll adjustment.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: rapid thermal dissipation (qE component of NPQ); photoprotection under fluctuating light
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: de-epoxidizes violaxanthin via antheraxanthin to zeaxanthin during high light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sunfleck-induced photo-oxidative stress; fluctuating high-light pulses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NPQ1 blocks zeaxanthin formation, preventing acclimatory increase in NPQ despite increased PsbS and causing stronger seed-yield reduction and reduced primary root growth under sunflecks.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. npq1 mutants have near-wild-type carotenoid composition in darkness but cannot form zeaxanthin through violaxanthin de-epoxidation. 2. Under sunflecks, npq1 increased violaxanthin-cycle pigment content and lutein but did not increase NPQ capacity, indicating zeaxanthin is required for acclimatory qE enhancement. 3. npq1 showed decreased primary root growth and the largest seed-yield reduction under long-term sunfleck stress.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for qE-associated photoprotection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; pigment HPLC; chlorophyll fluorescence NPQ kinetics; PsbS immunoblot; growth and seed-yield phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: without zeaxanthin, NPQ acclimation fails; stronger primary root growth inhibition and seed-yield reduction under stress
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study concludes that zeaxanthin synthesis by NPQ1 is necessary for sunfleck-induced NPQ up-regulation; extra lutein cannot substitute.
